monitor.notes: Vehicle Health card now shows pending + permanent fault codes.
Relays the Vehicle Health / inspection-readiness summary to the app.
Privacy: the car-ID feature now shows make + year only; the VIN is never transmitted.
Relays the sensor's VIN-decoded car identity to the app (new car-ID channel), so the app can auto-detect your car and suggest the matching gear profile.
Fixes a periodic stutter on the gauge: the on-screen log was blocking on the USB port when powered from a car adapter. All pages (especially Tuner) should be smoother now.
Tuner performance: the honeycomb dial now renders much faster — the fade and panel shading are precomputed instead of recalculated every frame, so the Tuner page is smoother (less stutter).

Anti-sleep: the reminder now only counts time driven above a speed you set (default 60 km/h), so being stuck in slow city traffic pauses the timer instead of either nagging you or resetting it. Set the speed in the Anti-sleep card.

New anti-sleep reminder: a full-screen “Take a break” alert after a set time of continuous driving (default 2h), reset by a real rest stop and snoozed by a tap. Turn it on/off and tune the timing in the app's Anti-sleep card. A drowsy-driving aid, not a substitute for real rest.

Tuner now has FOUR configurable slots instead of two: the RPM dot tacho, the energy arc, and a two-line readout (a big main value with a small second value above it). The gear can go in either readout line and shows both P/R/N/D and the calculated gear number.
Fixes the gear number vanishing on the Tuner page — it now shows the calculated gear (1-8) as well as P/R/N/D.
Tuner layering: the SN mark now sits behind the honeycomb as a watermark, and the energy arc is on top with rounded ends.
Tuner face: the empty background is now pure black, so the honeycomb sits on true black instead of a faint grey.

Tuner face fixes from the first real-screen photo: removed a leftover debug line at the bottom of the dial, and the honeycomb now fades to black toward the rim instead of filling the whole face. (The mesh colour itself is set by the Honeycomb swatch in the app.)
Tuner face, dialled in: bigger gear digit, readout lines with room to breathe, SN mark centred, the face fading to black at the rim, and the readout panel a touch darker.

Tuner face refinements: the gear is now the big number, the readout lines have room to breathe, and the SN mark sits at the centre of the dial. The main value you pick is now the one the pointer and the big number follow.

Fixes Tuner turning back into Hero when you saved it. Also carries the new Tuner gauge face from the Store and the shift light that beats instead of flickering. Your colours, layouts and car settings are kept.

New Tuner gauge face — an import-scene dial with a honeycomb background, a dot tacho and a floating pointer. It's a paid theme in the Store; the rest of the gauge is unchanged. The shift light now beats instead of flickering, at the fastest rate this screen can actually show. Your colours, layouts and car settings are kept.

The Needle page is rebuilt: the second value now gets its own small dial with its own scale, instead of a short hand sharing the main one — so both readings mean something. The main needle gains a counterweight tail. Every label and unit is also bigger across all pages (they were too small to read at a glance while driving).

Fix: when you change a page's style (e.g. to Ticks) in the app, the gauge now jumps to that page so you see the change — it no longer sits on the first page. Opening the app still doesn't move the gauge.

Adds a one-line calibration readout to the Last Drive card (menu → Session) after a hard pull, so wheel-horsepower can be tuned to match your car. Do one full-throttle pull, then open the Last Drive card and photograph the CAL line.
Fixes the STALE indicator flickering a few times right after you start the car. Best with the sensor (node) updated too. No change to your gauge layout.

New animated startup: gold ticks sweep around the ring and back like a car's gauge self-test, then the SN logo fades in and the whole thing dissolves into your gauge. Also fixes the white flash the screen showed for a moment on power-up — it now comes up black.

Fix: the gauge jumped to the first page the moment you started editing. Now it stays on whatever page it's showing while you edit — only swiping on the gauge changes the page. (v0.21.1 fixed one path but missed the per-field one.)

Fix: opening the app no longer bounces the gauge back to the first page. Live preview now updates the gauge where it is, without jumping pages, and only when you actually change something. (To preview changes on the bench with the car off, turn on DEMO in the gauge menu.)

Everything you change in the app now shows on the gauge as you do it — switch a page's style, swap a value, change a colour, add a page, flip to mph, set the car's weight: the gauge follows along instantly. Nothing is written to the gauge's memory until you tap Save, so you can experiment freely and back out.

Ticks can now show a second value: a short glowing tick rides just outside the ring, same thickness as the main ticks but brighter, in its own colour. The lit ticks now glide smoothly with the value instead of stepping. Number colours are yours to pick, separate from the tick colour. And the gauge is 3x faster (the arc glow was redrawing the whole arc four times). New DEMO mode in the menu runs a fake drive so you can show the gauge off with no car.

The Needle face can now show TWO values, like a clock — a long thin needle for the main one and a short one for the second, each with its own colour. And every page now lets you pick the colour of the number itself, on any of the four looks. Ticks stays one value. Note: this update resets your gauge layout once.

Everything glows now — the main arc and the bars get the same neon bloom as the Needle and Ticks faces, in your page colour (and red under the shift light). Plus you can now have up to 10 pages (was 6). Note: this update resets your gauge layout once.

Two new gauge looks — Needle and Ticks. Needle is a fine analog pointer with a neon glow on a pure-black face; Ticks is a ring that lights up right on your value. Both glow in your page colour and flash red with the shift light. Pick them per page in the app (Hero / Bars / Needle / Ticks).

Add or remove gauge pages — you can now have anywhere from 1 to 6 pages (was fixed at 4). Use the − / + under the page list in the app. Also the +/− split line on the gauge arc is a bit bolder. Note: this update resets your gauge layout once — set your pages again after updating.

Dyno peak-hold — the Power/Torque gauges now keep a bright 'drag pointer' at your best of the drive (on the arc + on bars), plus a PK reading under the number, like a real dyno. Resets when you switch the car off. Pairs with the v0.16.0 virtual dyno.

Virtual dyno — real Power (hp) and Torque (Nm) gauges that work on ANY car. It measures how hard the car actually accelerates (from speed) and, with your car's weight, computes power by physics — no special sensor needed, and it automatically adds up petrol + electric. Set your car's Weight (kg) in the app's Drivetrain page, then pick Power (dyno) / Torque (dyno) on any gauge. Note: this resets your gauge layout once (reconfigure your 4 pages).

Accelerator gauge + smarter 0-100 timer. New 'Accelerator (passive)' readout shows how hard the pedal is pressed (%), read straight off the car — no extra sensor. And the hidden 0-100 km/h timer now only starts on a real full-throttle launch (pedal ≥ 80%), so crawling in traffic won't trigger it. Update the sensor to v0.5.7 FIRST, then the gauge.

Choose your units — a new Units section in the app lets you pick km/h or mph, °C or °F, bar / psi / kPa, km or mi, km/L / mpg / L/100km, hp / kW / PS, Nm or lb-ft, L/h or gal/h. One tap for all-Metric or all-Imperial, or set each on its own. Also a new Fuel-economy gauge (instant km/L). The gauge converts on screen; your saved layout is kept. (Updating resets the display config to defaults once — just re-pick your pages.)

Power gauges (estimated) — three new channels you can put on any gauge: WHP (total wheel power), Engine (ICE) and Electric (EV). It works out power from the airflow and motor torque the car already reports, and adds them up. On a petrol-only car the electric part is simply 0, so WHP shows engine power. Pick them under 'Power' in the Gauge display. Numbers are estimates — fine-tune later against a known figure.

Arc zero divider — any gauge that can go negative (Motor torque, Boost, Timing, fuel trims) now shows a small tick on the arc at the zero point, so you can see the split between + and − (e.g. accelerating vs regen) at a glance.
New Motor torque channel — pick it in the app's Gauge display to see the electric motor's push/regen live in Nm. Needs node v0.5.6 (flash the sensor first).

Exhaust valve control — the gauge opens/closes your electric exhaust valve by replaying your remote's 433 MHz codes over a small TX module (wire it to GP5); no extra box. New Exhaust card in the app: Quiet/Auto/Loud + clone-the-remote setup. Auto opens when driven hard. Needs a 433 TX module + a one-time code sniff.

Per-car gear: the gauge now works out the gear from YOUR car's gearbox (the drivetrain you pick in the app), not a Volvo-only table — so gear is right on a Mazda2 and every other car. Pairs with node v0.5.5 + the new Mazda2/Demio presets. No change on the V60.
Bus capture (RE): a BUS LOG menu item + app 'Bus capture' card record EVERY CAN frame on the OBD port to a file you can download as a SavvyCAN CSV for offline reverse-engineering. Needs node v0.5.4.

Fresh diagnostic-probe capture each key-on: the gauge now nudges the sensor to re-run its WWH/freeze/0x19 gateway probe after boot, so the Node log captures it live (it used to run only once at the sensor's own boot, which rarely happens). Needs node v0.5.3.

Node log capture: the gauge now saves the sensor's diagnostic log (PID/DTC/gateway probes) to a file you can pull in the app's new 'Node log' card as a .txt — read in-car RE results offline, no laptop. Pairs with node v0.5.2's legislated probe.

Accel timer results are now SAVED: best time per target persists on the gauge (survives power-off), a 'BEST TIMES' board in the ACCEL menu, and the app's new 'Acceleration times' card reads them over BLE + exports CSV. Result card shows NEW BEST.

Accel timer: both modes now do a 5-second launch countdown (5..1 -> GO) so you can stage launch control, then timing triggers on speed as before; hold to cancel. Gear: the moment the car moves (speed > 0) it shows 1st gear right away.

Acceleration Timer menu: pick capture by SPEED (0-100 / 100-200 / 200-300 km/h) or by DISTANCE (60 ft / 201 m 1/8-mile / 402 m 1/4-mile, with trap speed). A chip shows on the gauge while it measures, then a result card. The auto 0-60/0-100 popup stays, with bigger numbers.

Richer fault-code card in the app: plain-English explainer per code — severity, the symptoms you'll actually feel, drive-ability advice, and the most likely causes ranked. Monitor also reports current RPM/coolant/load with the codes. Needs node v0.5.1.

Fault codes in the app: the Fault-code card now reads your car's ACTUAL stored codes over BLE (new char 7e1c020a) and explains each one, with a Clear-codes button (runs Mode-04 on the car). Needs node v0.5.1.
Bigger code-meaning text on the check-engine alarm (larger, easier to read at a glance).
Clear fault codes from the gauge: menu -> DTC, when codes are stored swipe up to arm, tap to confirm -> the car's codes + check-engine light are cleared (node Mode-04). Needs node v0.5.1.

CHECK-ENGINE alarm meaning now shown in ENGLISH (short per-code label, e.g. 'Cylinder misfire', 'Catalyst efficiency low'), drawn with the built-in font. Same severity ring + per-code icon + code text as v0.10.0. Needs node v0.5.0 for the actual codes.

Enriched CHECK-ENGINE alarm: node now sends the REAL stored codes (0xAF) so the full-screen alarm shows a severity ring (red=serious / amber=emissions), a per-code icon (P0301 -> spark plug), the TRANSLATED Thai meaning, and the code text. Needs node v0.5.0 for the actual codes; falls back to count-only without it.

Check-engine full-screen alert. A stored fault code now raises the same full-screen amber alarm as the coolant/fuel warnings (CHECK ENGINE + the code count + TAP TO DISMISS), so you can't miss it. Dismiss it and it stays quiet until the code is actually cleared; a small badge still marks it on the gauge. Includes the low-speed gear fix.

Low-speed gear tuning. The speed below which the gauge simply holds 1st gear (while the torque converter is slipping) is now 8 km/h. This build also includes the full gear-flicker fix (debounce + low-speed hold) and the on-gauge CHECK ENGINE badge, in case your gauge had not received those yet.

Check-engine warning on the gauge. When your car reports a stored fault code, the gauge now shows a small amber CHECK ENGINE badge (with the code count) at the bottom automatically - you no longer have to open the menu to notice. Five taps still opens the CODES list, which now explains 1800+ generic codes in plain language. Includes the low-speed gear fix (v0.9.11).

Gear at low speed. Below ~20 km/h the torque converter slips, so the calculated gear couldn't reliably tell 1st from 2nd. It now simply holds 1st at low speed (where you're launching anyway) and resolves the real gear once the converter locks up above that — no more 1-2 flicker off the line. Includes the smoother-gear debounce (v0.9.10) and Last Drive fix (v0.9.8).

Smoother gear readout. The calculated gear could flicker (1-2-1-2 at low speed while the torque converter slips, or 3-4-5 when you lift off after a kickdown) because the digit is derived from RPM vs speed. It now waits for a gear to settle before showing a change - downshifts stay quick, upshifts are held slightly longer so a throttle lift no longer makes the number jump around. Includes the Last Drive save fix (v0.9.8).

Last Drive fix. Your gauge is powered from the 12V accessory socket, so it switches off the instant the car does — before it could save the drive you just finished, which is why the Last Drive page was stuck at zero. It now saves your drive progress every minute while you drive, so the recap survives the power cut and shows your latest drive at the next key-on. Includes the live colour preview (v0.9.7) and CVT display (v0.9.6).

Live colour preview. When you change a page's arc colour in the app, the gauge now recolours that page instantly while you drag the picker — it jumps to the page you're editing and updates in real time, so you can dial in the exact colour on the screen itself. The colour saves normally when you tap Save. Includes the CVT display (v0.9.6) and the multi-car gear tools + 280+ car Drivetrain library (v0.9.5).

CVT display. If you pick a CVT / eCVT car in the app's Drivetrain list, the gauge now shows "CVT" in the gear slot instead of a made-up number — a CVT has no fixed gears, so a calculated gear was meaningless. Your real P/R/N/D (from an AXIS sensor or knob) still takes priority when available; everything else works as before. Also adds the multi-car gear-profile push and the 180+ car Drivetrain preset library from v0.9.5.

True gear readout. Your gauge now shows the real P / R / N / D / B position taken straight from the car, so the letter on screen always matches your actual shifter instead of being guessed from speed and revs. In Drive it also shows the current ratio (1–8). Includes the smarter neutral and EV detection from v0.9.3, and everything from v0.9.2 (car HEALTH check + electric-vs-engine drive split).

Car HEALTH check + PHEV energy split. New HEALTH item in the on-gauge menu (five quick taps → HEALTH) shows a one-glance verdict — ALL GOOD / WATCH / ATTENTION — with a checklist: engine temps, 12V battery, fuel level, stored fault codes, and fuel trim, each marked OK / WATCH / CHECK (or '--' when the car isn't reporting that reading yet). And the Last-drive recap now shows how much of the drive was electric vs engine — an 'EV 62%  ICE 38%' line — for the plug-in hybrid. Both reuse data already on the link; no new setup. Everything from v0.9.1 (drive-log export) included.

Drive log export. When you record a drive on the gauge (tap RECORD in its menu), you can now pull that recording into the app and save it as a spreadsheet (CSV) — every reading (RPM, speed, boost, coolant, battery, gear, throttle, and more) sampled five times a second, with a timestamp. Open the new 'Drive log' card in the app, tap Download CSV, then open it in Excel / Numbers / Google Sheets or any data tool. An Erase button clears the gauge's stored log. Nothing else changes — everything from v0.9.0 (knob-gear ecosystem, sensor pairing, RPM-arc-red, gear/arc refinements, gamma dimming, engine-off fuel fix) is included.

AXIS Knob ecosystem + gear upgrades. If you have an AXIS shift knob, the gauge now shows its real engaged gear wherever you placed a gear reading on a page — including P/R/N/D, which the calculated gear can't show. No setup: the gauge finds the knob on its own and only your gear slot changes; every page you built stays exactly as you set it (if a page has no gear reading, the knob simply isn't shown). New PAIR option in the menu locks the gauge to your own sensor (and knob) with a 6-digit confirmation code, so it reads only your car and ignores another AXIS nearby. The RPM arc now fades toward red as the revs climb, whatever colour you picked. If a page's main value is the gear, its arc follows the second value instead of the meaningless gear digit. When you're stopped the calculated gear always resets to 1, so wheelspin or traction control can't leave it stuck in a high gear. Screen colours no longer shift when the display dims, and a low-fuel warning can't linger after you switch the car off. Includes everything from v0.8.1 (touch overhaul, on-gauge menu + drive recorder, brightness).

Touch overhaul + menu polish. Fixes the big one: the gauge's touch controller was decoding finger coordinates from the wrong register bytes, so every swipe was garbage — taps worked but swipes flip-flopped. Now swipes are clean and gestures are split up: swipe LEFT/RIGHT changes the gauge page, five quick taps open the menu, swipe UP/DOWN moves one item at a time with a smooth AXIS-knob slide, tap selects, press-and-hold exits. Choosing RECORD starts logging and drops you back to the gauge with the red dot. Brightness now caps at a safe level (no more tearing at max) and draws a clean solid arc. A brief settle after opening the menu stops a stray tap from selecting. Everything from v0.8.0 (menu + drive recorder) included.

On-gauge menu + drive recorder. Press and hold the screen to open a clean mono-line menu (swipe up/down to move, tap to choose): RECORD, DTC SCAN, SESSION, BRIGHTNESS. RECORD logs your whole drive to the gauge — a small red dot shows it's live; it saves automatically when you switch off (download & CSV/GPX export land in the next app update). DTC SCAN shows your car's stored fault-code count (green tick when clear). SESSION reopens your last-drive recap. BRIGHTNESS lets you dim/brighten right on the gauge. The old hold-to-log diagnostic screen is retired. Everything from v0.7.4 (accel timer, engine-off fix, Phase-B channels) unchanged.

Acceleration timer. From a standing start the gauge now times your run and flashes a 0-60 / 0-100 km/h result card, then remembers your best — a green 'NEW BEST!' when you beat it. No setup: it arms when you're stopped, starts when you launch, and shows the splits. Tap to dismiss. Uses the speed already on the sensor link, so nothing else changes. Everything from v0.7.3 (engine-off alert fix) unchanged.

No more false alerts after you switch off. When the engine goes off the sensor now tells the gauge instantly, so the gauge closes cleanly instead of holding the last reading — which sometimes flashed a false LOW FUEL (or other warning) on a car that was already off. Warnings also only trigger on fresh live data now, and any on-screen alarm clears the moment the link drops. Pair with sensor v0.4.5. Everything from v0.7.2 (the Phase-B channels) unchanged.

New Phase-B readings on the gauge. Pairs with sensor v0.4.4 to decode and display six more channels your car quietly answers: measured air-fuel ratio / lambda (the real wideband value, distinct from the commanded one), absolute engine load, accelerator-pedal position, fuel-rail pressure, relative throttle, and a fault-code (DTC) count that flags amber if your car has stored an emissions code. Add any of them to a gauge page in the app. Everything from v0.7.x (Last-drive recap, rim-halo shift-light, safety alerts) unchanged. Update the sensor to v0.4.4 as well so these channels actually arrive.

Drive-recap tidy-up: on cars whose OBD port doesn't report fuel flow (like this V60 T8, where fuel data is gated off), the recap card no longer claims 'EV drive' — it just shows your battery swing. Everything else from v0.7.0 (the Last-drive card) unchanged.

Drive recap. When you switch off, the gauge remembers your drive; the next time you start the car it greets you with a 'Last drive' card — distance, time, top speed, max RPM, peak boost, peak water temp, plus your battery swing and fuel used. It shows for a few seconds (tap to skip) then the gauge takes over. A real overheat/low-fuel alarm still comes through instantly, even over the recap. Includes v0.6.2's rim-halo shift-light and v0.6.1's faster updates + v0.6.0 alerts.

New shift-light look. When you pass your set shift RPM the gauge now glows a pulsing red ring around the rim and the RPM figure flashes red — on a black screen, so the readout stays crisp — instead of flooding the whole screen red. Elegant, and it won't smear on the display. Includes v0.6.1's faster Bluetooth updates and the v0.6.0 safety alerts.

Faster updates + a smoother shift light. Firmware updates now transfer several times faster over Bluetooth (the gauge receives them in batches instead of one small piece at a time). The shift-light — the full-screen red flash at your set shift RPM — is now a smooth pulse that fills the whole screen, instead of stuttering in from the top. Includes the v0.6.0 safety alerts (coolant/oil/cat overheat, low 12 V, low fuel).

Safety alerts — the gauge now watches your engine even while you're looking at another page. If coolant, oil, or catalyst temperature runs too hot, the 12 V battery drops too low, or the tank is nearly empty, a full-screen warning flashes over whatever page you're on, showing the value and how urgent it is. Tap to dismiss: a real overheat comes back if it's still too hot, while a low-fuel warning stays quiet until you refuel. It ignores brief bad sensor readings and, on the hybrid, doesn't false-alarm on oil/cat temps while the car runs on electric. Includes everything from v0.5.x — the smooth gold gradient arc, iPhone-fast tap, and the page-change fade.

AXIS Monitor is HERO-only: 4 single-value pages (tap to cycle) — TURBO / RPM / COOLANT / BATTERY — each one big arc + value with the smooth dithered gradient (flat edge, 10→100% fill, 13pt readout). No BARS or 5-arc pages. (Supersedes v0.5.2/0.5.1; the withdrawn v0.5.0 had a display/touch pin regression.)

New A/F (air-fuel ratio) channel — place it on any page as 'A/F' (10–20) or as 'λ' lambda (0.70–1.30). It's the ECU's commanded/target mixture (from OBD PID 0x44), so it reads ~14.6 at cruise and richens under boost/full throttle; the gauge hides it automatically if your car doesn't report it. Also: gear now auto-detects the gearbox (Aisin AWF8 vs ZF 8HP) for a more accurate calculated gear. Includes the v0.2 release: working screen brightness, per-page arc colour + redline, all OBD channels, and wireless (OTA) updates.

node.notes: VIN auto-ID now recognizes Porsche + Mini/Land Rover/Jaguar/Tesla/Mitsubishi/Suzuki/Peugeot/Citroen/Renault/Fiat/Alfa/Jeep/Ram (were Unknown).
Vehicle Health now also reads pending + permanent fault codes (early-warning + must-fix-to-pass-inspection).
Vehicle Health: reads the emissions readiness monitors so the app can show a 'ready for inspection' check.
Privacy: the VIN is never sent or logged — the sensor identifies your car's make + year only.
Sends the decoded car make/year/VIN to the gauge so the app can auto-identify your car (and suggest the right gear profile). Needs a real car to verify.
VIN auto-ID: reads the car's VIN and logs the make + model year (e.g. 'CAR: BMW 2013'). First step toward the app auto-picking your car profile with no manual setup. Needs a real car to verify.

Reads more cars: adds physical OBD addressing (0x7E0) as a fallback for gatewayed cars that ignore the generic broadcast request. Pairs with the diagnostic build to close the 'no values' gap on brands like BMW. No change on the Volvo.
Adds a wireless bring-up diagnostic so a car that shows no values (e.g. a BMW) can be diagnosed in-car via the app log — no laptop needed. Logging only; no behaviour change on the Volvo.
Removes a leftover diagnostic probe that kept querying the car every time it connected, even with the engine off. Less battery use and less needless talking to the car's gateway when parked.
Battery + gateway fix: the sensor now stops talking to the car when it's parked or charging instead of polling continuously with the engine off. It stays fully awake while driving (including EV).

Reverts the recent change that could leave the gauge showing only rpm/speed — back to the previous behaviour where all engine values stream (the small 'STALE' blink at start-up may return; that's the trade-off). If values are STILL missing after this, it's the car's gateway needing a key-cycle, not the sensor.

Recovers the engine-data connection faster. If the car's gateway briefly stops answering (can happen after several updates in a row), the sensor now keeps re-testing and the engine values (coolant, boost, battery, etc.) come back on their own within about a minute. Also logs why data is missing (engine off / EV, vs gateway busy).
Keeps sending live data to the gauge even while it runs its start-up self-checks, so the gauge no longer blips 'STALE' a few times when you start the car. Recommended update.

Reads the accelerator pedal straight off the car's bus (a free passive signal) and sends it to the gauge — powers the new Accelerator readout and the full-throttle launch detection for the 0-100 timer. Flash this BEFORE the gauge v0.15.0.

Motor torque: reads the electric-drive torque off the car's CAN bus (RE'd) and sends it so the gauge can show a live Motor torque readout (positive when accelerating, negative during regen braking). No change to anything else.

Universal decode — works on ANY OBD-II car, not just the Volvo. Fixes RPM/speed reading 0 after moving the node to a Mazda2 (they were sourced only from Volvo-specific CAN broadcasts): now falls back to standard OBD PIDs when those broadcasts are absent, and trusts the car's own PID support list so the scan is fast + clean on every car. No change on the V60.
Raw CAN capture: on a 0xC3 command the sensor switches to listen-only and forwards every CAN frame (0xCA) to the gauge for the bus-capture RE feature. Off by default; normal telemetry untouched.

On-demand diagnostic probe (0xC2): re-runs the WWH/freeze/0x19 + DTC scan when the gauge asks (it was one-shot at boot; the sensor runs on constant OBD power so it rarely reboots). Pairs with monitor v0.12.4 for a fresh capture each drive.

In-car diagnostic probe (read-only, no security): after the engine answers, tries WWH-OBD (0x22 F4xx), Mode-02 freeze-frame, and 0x19 Read-DTC-Info, logging what the car's gateway passes. Results appear on the monitor's USB serial. One-time capture — turn off after.
Clear DTC support: on a 0xC1 command from the gauge, runs Mode-04 (wipes stored codes + turns the MIL off) then rescans. Also the v0.5.0 DTC broadcast. USB-flashed on min_spiffs (OTA-capable).

DTC BROADCAST: reads Mode-03 stored codes and broadcasts the real 2-byte codes (new 0xAF packet) so the gauge alarm can show per-code severity/icon/Thai meaning. Baseline scan after ECM lock, then rescans every 20s while codes are present (a later 0-count scan clears the alarm). BEST flashed over USB (new packet).

Sensor firmware catch-up. Brings the published sensor firmware in line with what is installed (true gear selector, multi-car gear profiles, timer-only deep-sleep, and the anti-flood polling backoff). Built on a dual-OTA partition so future sensor updates can go wireless.

The real fix for the false LOW FUEL after you switch off. When the engine stops answering (car off / parked) the sensor now marks its fuel/coolant/etc. readings as 'no data' instead of holding the last value while it keeps broadcasting — so the gauge can't raise a false LOW FUEL (or other) warning on the stale reading during the ~90 s before the sensor sleeps. Works with any display version. Supersedes v0.4.5 (whose 'sleeping' notice fired too late, only at sleep).

Tells the gauge the moment the engine goes off. Just before the sensor goes to sleep (engine off / car parked) it now sends a quick 'sleeping' notice so the gauge closes instantly and drops any warning — no more false LOW FUEL lingering on the last reading after you switch off. Pair with display v0.7.3. Everything from v0.4.4 (Phase-B channels) unchanged.

Phase B — reads the last of the hidden channels. On top of v0.4.3's unlocked set, the sensor now also decodes measured (wideband) air-fuel ratio, absolute engine load, accelerator-pedal position, fuel-rail pressure, relative throttle, and the stored fault-code count. Pair with display v0.7.2 to show them; pick any as a gauge in the app. No pause at startup (the diagnostic scan stays off).

Unlocks the hidden readings. Your car's OBD port quietly answers far more sensors than it officially lists — its support list admitted only 3, but a full probe found 40 readable. This build stops trusting that under-reported list and reads the whole verified set directly, so channels that were silently blank now come alive: fuel level, fuel rate (used for the drive recap), catalyst temperature, intake & ambient air temp, airflow (MAF), engine load, throttle, spark timing, fuel trims, run time and trip distance — pick any of them for a gauge page in the app. Oil temperature genuinely isn't offered by this car, so it stays hidden. The one-time diagnostic scan from v0.4.2 is now switched off (no more ~10 s pause at each start).

DIAGNOSTIC build: on the first engine-on after installing, the sensor probes every OBD reading and reports which ones your car actually answers — usually more than the car admits to supporting. Look at the gauge's diagnostic log (press and hold the screen) a few seconds after starting the engine to see the list. This build pauses telemetry ~10 s at each start while it scans; switch back to a normal build once you've captured the list.

Fixes wireless (over-the-air) updates to the sensor, which were failing with a 'write error'. The sensor now saves the incoming firmware safely in the background instead of during the radio callback, matching how the display already updates itself. NOTE: because this fix lives in the sensor's own update receiver, this one version has to be installed by USB cable — after that, all future sensor updates go wirelessly as normal. Includes the v0.4.0 battery-save fix (engine-off deep sleep, <10 V threshold, instant CAN-wake) and everything from v0.3.x (A/F channel, gearbox auto-detect).

Battery-save fix: the sensor now recognises the engine-off state (the car's control-module voltage reads ~0.2 V when parked) and goes to deep sleep, so it no longer keeps the 12 V battery awake while the car sits. The off threshold is <10 V; it wakes instantly the moment the car's CAN bus comes alive (ignition/unlock). Includes everything from v0.3.x — the A/F (air-fuel) channel, gearbox auto-detect, and instant CAN-wake reconnect.

Adds the A/F (air-fuel ratio) reading — the sensor now decodes the ECU's commanded mixture (OBD PID 0x44) so the gauge can show A/F or lambda. Gear calculation now auto-detects the gearbox: it fits both an Aisin AWF8 and a ZF 8HP ratio table against live rpm÷speed and locks onto whichever matches your car. Includes v0.2.2's instant CAN-wake reconnect + no-drain sleep.
